name: The Narrative Liaison
focus: Combining V7's safe boundaries with V5's patient-centered storytelling style.

action_taxonomy.goal: Establish early alignment and confirm referral logistics

action_items: Confirm insurance eligibility (Straight Medicare vs. Advantage)
Verify diagnosis meets home care criteria
primary_intents: Ensure qualification before introducing services

action_items: Gather DOB, phone, address, and POA details
Check distance for Medical Assistant (MA) travel
Audit face sheets for address errors (cross-check Atlantis data against facility face sheet before HHA referral)
Audit Atlantis demographics (verify insurance, address, phone #, and DOB accuracy in Atlantis queue)
primary_intents: Readiness for immediate family communication

action_items: Meet patient in-person within first 48 hours
Shadow the Traditional Provider (TP) to get facility entry
primary_intents: Gain trust and prepare patient for transition

action_items: Contact Social Worker (SW) and TP immediately upon assignment
Confirm referral receipt with HHA intake coordinator
Verify internal 'Hub' receipt (confirm the referral was received and logged in the Hub system)
primary_intents: Maintain relationship for early discharge info

action_items: Verify that the V-Card was successfully inserted into the patient's phone
Ask patient/family: 'When you see this number, that's us — Healing Partners'
Document confirmation in v_card_flyer_status
primary_intents: Ensure the patient recognizes Healing Partners calls (Caller ID only)
The V-Card is NOT for coordinating logistics

action_items: Ask patient/family: 'How ready do you feel about going home?'
Ask patient/family: 'What concerns do you have about managing at home?'
Document the family's sentiment (confident, anxious, reluctant)
If anxious or reluctant, flag to SW for a care conference
primary_intents: Gauge family readiness without performing clinical intake
Route anxiety-driven barriers to the SW for professional intervention

action_items: Audit clinical notes shared by the SW to confirm the patient meets wound care program eligibility
Document qualification status in Atlantis
If eligibility is unclear, flag to SW for clinical confirmation
primary_intents: Ensure program eligibility is verified early without performing clinical assessment
Route clinical eligibility questions to the SW

action_taxonomy.goal: Track progress, educate family, and escalate barriers to the appropriate professional

action_items: Track discharge timeline updates relayed by SW or facility liaison
Note wound care progress from provider updates shared by the SW
primary_intents: Ensure awareness of barriers or changes in progress without accessing clinical systems directly

action_items: Maintain weekly communication with staff/SW
Request post-conference summary from SW regarding discharge timeline changes
primary_intents: Ensure immediate updates if discharge plans shift

action_items: Educate patient/family on program benefits
Discuss POA awareness of home services
Walk family through Day 1 expectations at home: who arrives, what equipment should be present, and what to do if the HHA nurse is late
Insert V-Card into patient's phone and deliver program flyer to patient/family
primary_intents: Prevent 'missed' discharge or refusal
Reduce caregiver anxiety by setting concrete expectations

action_items: Learn PCP and HHA preferences well in advance
Identify family support/training needs
primary_intents: Proactive transition planning

action_items: Escalate clinical or insurance concerns to the Social Worker rather than attempting resolution directly
Flag volatile family situations to SW for immediate intervention
Escalate weekend coverage gaps to the SW (flag Friday discharges with no weekend HHA coverage so SW can adjust the discharge date or arrange bridging)
primary_intents: Ensure complex issues are handled by the appropriate professional

action_items: Ask the SW to include the PN in a family update call or meeting
During the meeting, educate family on Day 1 expectations and answer non-clinical questions
Document meeting outcomes and any new concerns flagged by the family
primary_intents: Gain direct access to family sentiment without overstepping SW's role
Ensure family education happens in a supported, collaborative setting

action_items: Keep a running log in Atlantis of each 'waiting' phase (waiting for HHA acceptance, waiting for SW response, waiting for DME delivery)
Update the log each time a status change occurs or a flag is sent to the SW
Use the timeline to provide context when escalating delays
primary_intents: Create an auditable trail of the PN's waiting periods
Demonstrate proactive monitoring without vendor contact

action_taxonomy.goal: Deliver a 'Maximum Reward' handoff and secure service adoption

action_items: Verify with SW the confirmed D/C date and selected HHA before coordinating logistics
Confirm no address/contact changes
primary_intents: Ensure alignment with all transition providers

action_items: Pass contact/address info and first appointment availability to home team
Verify HHA has confirmed the start-of-care date, assigned nurse name, and expected first-visit time window
primary_intents: Prepare home team for continuity of care

action_items: Conduct 24-hour pre-discharge pulse call to patient/family to confirm home readiness and first-visit time
Hand physical appointment card with Care Coordinator info to patient
Confirm time works for patient/DM
Schedule the Medical Assistant (MA) for the first visit within 24 hours of discharge
primary_intents: Ensure patient feels informed and comfortable

action_items: If HHA is not confirmed ready, flag the status to SW in Atlantis and WAIT
Do NOT call HHA intake, DME vendors, or transport companies directly
Document what was flagged and the SW's response
If SW has not responded, ask SW for an update — do not attempt to resolve independently
primary_intents: Enforce the 'Wait' default — PN flags, SW resolves
Prevent the PN from crossing into vendor communication

action_items: Check Atlantis portal for HHA acceptance status (do NOT call HHA directly)
If portal shows HHA accepted with SOC date, proceed to schedule MA
If portal shows pending or no update, flag to SW and WAIT
Document portal status in ma_visit_booking
primary_intents: Verify HHA readiness through system data, not direct vendor contact
Maintain the No-Vendor Rule while ensuring MA is not scheduled prematurely

action_items: Coordinate with Regional Director (RD) and HHA to prevent same-day 'Double Dipping'
primary_intents: Ensure service payment and coverage safety

friction_taxonomy.logical_dna_rule: The process is historically slow because the caseworker is required to obtain three separate contractor estimates to determine the company for installation (e.g., wheelchair ramps).
In regions with only one specialist, illness triggers a care gap; the PN must coordinate with HHA generalist nurses to bridge dressing changes until a specialist is available.
To qualify for specialized home wound care, the patient must require at least one other skilled service (RN or PT); losing these services renders them ineligible even if the wound is open.
The HHA intake department is frozen due to staffing capacity, holiday, weekend, or system outage; no new admissions are being processed regardless of patient readiness.

Durable Medical Equipment (hospital bed, O2 concentrator) was confirmed delivered but the wrong item arrived, or the vendor delivered to the wrong address; the PN must verify equipment at the home before the HHA start-of-care visit.
The single HHA intake coordinator is out sick, on PTO, or overwhelmed with referrals; the PN cannot confirm the admission slot or start-of-care date until intake processes the referral.
The primary caregiver has not been trained on wound care, catheter maintenance, or medication administration; discharge cannot proceed safely until the family demonstrates competency.

Critical handoff data (medication list, allergy list, or POA contact information) from the facility does not match the HHA's records; the PN must flag discrepancies to both parties before the first home visit.
The HHA requires a home safety evaluation (grab bars, ramp, clear pathways) before start-of-care; if the home fails inspection, modifications must be arranged before the patient can return.
The HHA does not have a nurse or aide available in the patient's zip code; the PN must find an alternative agency or negotiate coverage with a more distant branch.
The patient's preferred or nearest HHA is out-of-network for their insurance plan; the PN must identify an in-network agency willing to take the referral or suggest a single-case agreement to the SW.

Family members disagree about whether the patient should go home vs. a facility; the PN must educate and de-escalate while staying within scope (not making clinical recommendations or overriding the family's right to choose).

Medical transport (wheelchair van, stretcher transport) is unavailable or unscheduled for the discharge date; the PN must coordinate with transport vendors and confirm timing with the HHA's first-visit schedule.

The physician's home health orders were not faxed, were incomplete, or were sent to the wrong HHA; the PN must flag the missing orders to the SW and verify receipt before start-of-care can be scheduled.
HHA operates on reduced holiday staffing; admissions and first visits are suspended over holiday weekends, pushing the start-of-care date out regardless of patient readiness.

The primary caregiver or family member threatens to refuse discharge or demands the patient stay in the facility, driven by fear of managing care at home; the PN must educate and de-escalate while staying within scope (not making clinical promises or overriding the family's right to refuse).

The patient's record appears in the Atlantis queue with stale or missing data (wrong address, outdated insurance, missing phone number); the PN must audit the face sheet against Atlantis and request corrections before the HHA can process the referral.
Patient is discharged on a Friday but the HHA does not staff weekend admissions; the first home visit cannot occur until Monday at earliest, leaving a 48-72 hour gap in post-discharge monitoring.

The HHA nurse assigned to the first post-discharge home visit does not appear at the scheduled time; the PN must escalate to the HHA intake coordinator and flag the no-show to the SW to determine if the patient is safe.

The SW determines the patient's goal is Long-Term Care (LTC), not home discharge; the PN must perform a Neutral_LTC_Closure in Atlantis and stop all home transition work. Continuing to pitch home services to an LTC patient is an Overstep.

The PN cannot reach the patient or family during the mandatory 24-hour pre-discharge pulse call; without confirming home readiness and the first-visit time, the discharge proceeds with an unverified transition plan.

The HHA's start-of-care (SOC) scheduling conflicts with another patient admission or an internal staffing constraint; the first visit cannot be booked in the 24-hour window post-discharge, risking the PN's success metric.

The HHA has received the referral but has not yet confirmed acceptance (no SOC date, no assigned nurse, no first-visit window); the PN checks Atlantis portal for status, flags the delay to the SW, and WAITS. The PN does NOT call HHA intake directly.

The patient or family expresses anxiety, reluctance, or low confidence about managing care at home; the PN documents the sentiment score and flags it to the SW for a care conference. The PN does NOT perform clinical intake or assessment — sentiment verification only.

The family expresses anxiety specifically because the facility has not provided training on clinical tasks they will need to perform at home (wound dressing changes, catheter care, medication management); the PN documents the anxiety and flags the training gap to the SW for a care conference or bedside teaching session.

The PN is excluded from a key transition meeting (family conference, discharge planning huddle) and only learns of plan changes after the fact; the PN must request inclusion through the SW and update Atlantis with any new information received.

outcome_taxonomy.primary_exit_trigger: Surgeon sign-off and identification of skilled need (RN/PT)
outcome_taxonomy.leading_indicator_of_failure: Patient stabilizes clinically but has no support system or reliable caregiver at home

outcome_taxonomy.primary_exit_trigger: Completion of home modification (e.g., wheelchair ramp or chair lift installation)
outcome_taxonomy.leading_indicator_of_failure: Waiver estimates are rejected, or the Medicaid approval process is delayed indefinitely

outcome_taxonomy.primary_exit_trigger: Day 101 Medicare benefit exhaustion or Social Worker safety pivot
outcome_taxonomy.leading_indicator_of_failure: Family verbalizes an 'inability to help at home' or demonstrates unwillingness to assist
outcome_taxonomy.v5_outcome_mapping: Neutral_LTC_Closure — LTC pivot is a job requirement, NOT a failure. PN logs neutral closure in Atlantis.

outcome_taxonomy.primary_exit_trigger: Acute medical emergency (e.g., acute CHF or respiratory failure)
outcome_taxonomy.leading_indicator_of_failure: Evidence of O2 tank mismanagement, cellulitis flare-up, or missed HHA monitoring visits
outcome_taxonomy.v5_outcome_mapping: Failure_Transition_Breakdown — The home transition failed; patient returned to acute care.

outcome_taxonomy.primary_exit_trigger: First home visit completed within 24–48 hours of discharge with our services
outcome_taxonomy.leading_indicator_of_failure: Competitive steering by Social Worker or failure to establish trust during the intake phase
outcome_taxonomy.v5_outcome_mapping: Success_Home_with_First_Visit — Patient discharges home with our services AND the first post-discharge home visit is completed.

outcome_taxonomy.primary_exit_trigger: Patient or SW selects a different home health agency
outcome_taxonomy.leading_indicator_of_failure: PN failed to build rapport or HHA could not staff the case in the patient's area
outcome_taxonomy.v5_outcome_mapping: Neutral_Alternative_Agency — Patient chose a different agency. This is a job requirement, NOT a failure.
